---
title: "Accessing the data and setting up R"
---

This page gathers everything you need to follow the lab on your own machine: the data download, where to find the code, and how to get R set up if you haven't already.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1. Download the lab data

The full dataset вЂ” the pre-computed satellite damage raster, the operational zone polygons, the buildings shapefile, and the synthetic household survey вЂ” lives in a Dropbox folder:

::: callout-tip
## Download link

[**Lab data on Dropbox**](https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fo/z1ax4o17tfs8piohwn73m/AME7WpX6dJbixtmWqUFykwA?rlkey=i10gdfs2hfm5qazkf0sm9uw8s&st=r3s6jmvy&dl=0)

вљ пёЏ **This link expires on [24 May 2026].** If you're reading this after that date, email me for a refreshed link.
:::

Once downloaded:

1.  Unzip the folder.
2.  Move the contents into a `data/` folder inside your lab project directory. The expected structure is:

```         
your-project/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ lab_part1.qmd
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ lab_part2.qmd
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ lab_part2-3.qmd
в””в”Ђв”Ђ data/
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ geo/
    в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ raster_rf_damage.shp        (and siblings)
    в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ buildings_damaged.shp       (and siblings)
    в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ beirut_port_explosions_operational_zones_139.shp (and siblings)
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ maxar_processed/
    в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ MX_31Jul_5Aug_UN.tif
    в””в”Ђв”Ђ kr_rb_mm_synthetic.xlsx
```

3.  Open the `.qmd` files in RStudio. The relative paths (`data/geo/...`) in the code will resolve automatically.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2. Code and lab materials

All lab materials вЂ” the rendered Quarto pages, the source `.qmd` files, the slides, and any updates after the session вЂ” are hosted at:

::: callout-tip
## Course website

[**pietrostefani.github.io/man-made-disasters**](https://pietrostefani.github.io/man-made-disasters/)
:::

The site is updated as the labs evolve, so if you're returning to the material later, it's worth checking back for the latest version.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3. Setting up R

If you haven't installed R, RStudio, or the spatial-analysis packages used in these labs, the most reliable starting point is the Geographic Data Science setup guide:

::: callout-tip
## R installation and setup

[**gdsl-ul.github.io/gds/environR.html**](https://gdsl-ul.github.io/gds/environR.html)
:::

That guide will walk you through installing R, RStudio, and the core packages. The lab assumes you can already load `sf`, `terra`, `tidyverse`, `tmap`, and a handful of mapping helpers вЂ” the В§2.2 setup chunk lists them all explicitly.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 4. Downloading specific files from GitHub

If you only need a few files from a GitHub repo (rather than cloning the whole thing), there's a neat free tool that lets you download a single folder directly:

1.  Go to **<https://download-directory.github.io>**
2.  Find the folder you need on GitHub and copy its URL вЂ” for example: `https://github.com/pietrostefani/gds/tree/main/data/London`
3.  Paste it into the green box on download-directory and wait a few seconds. The site builds you a zip on the fly.
4.  Check your `Downloads` folder and unzip.

::: callout-warning
## Don't download the whole repository

It's tempting to hit the green "Code в†’ Download ZIP" button on GitHub and pull the entire repo, but this gets messy fast: you'll end up with hundreds of files you don't need, rendered HTML output that conflicts with your own `.qmd` files when you re-render, and a `data/` folder structure that may not match what the lab expects. Stick to grabbing just the folder you need using the method above.
:::

This is much faster than cloning the whole repository, and it's the easiest way to grab individual lab folders from the course materials.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 5. Troubleshooting

A few common issues and what to do about them:

**`Error in CPL_read_ogr(...) : Cannot open ...`** вЂ” the path is wrong. The lab uses relative paths from your project root (e.g. `data/geo/buildings_damaged.shp`). Either run the code from RStudio with the project open, or use `here::here("data/geo/buildings_damaged.shp")` to anchor the path to the project root.

**`Error: package 'sf' could not be loaded`** вЂ” `sf` needs system libraries (GDAL, GEOS, PROJ) that aren't installed by default on all systems. The setup guide linked above covers this.

**Tiles don't appear in the `tmap` interactive maps** вЂ” usually an internet hiccup with the basemap provider. Refresh, or switch the basemap line to `tm_basemap("OpenStreetMap")` which tends to be the most reliable.

**`mean_BLASTav` is `NA` for every zone** вЂ” the survey `bld_nbr` didn't join to the buildings shapefile. Check that both are integer/character types (not factor) before joining, and that you're using the *flipped* `BLASTav` from the load step.
